html:
[213]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 生知其异人，不相惊，屏息自退 - The protagonist recognized the old man as extraordinary and did not startle him. He held his breath and quietly retreated.
  • 诘朝，治具延之，欲求其术 - The next day, he prepared a meal and invited the old man, wanting to learn his method.
  • 君飞黄有日，不宜效江湖餔餟者流 - The old man knew the protagonist's intentions and said, 'You will achieve great success one day, but you should not follow the example of those who live off the land or in society.'
  • 老夫不能无报 - Although, I cannot remain ungrateful. I will give you something as a gift, but not yet.
  • 举以相赠，今尚不暇 - I will give you something as a gift, but not yet.
  • 叟诳我 - The old man is deceiving me.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 生 - Refers to the protagonist, likely a young man or scholar.
  • 叟 - Refers to an old man, often a wise or experienced figure.
  • 飞黄有日 - A metaphor for achieving great success, often used in classical Chinese literature.
  • 江湖餔餟者流 - Refers to people who live off the land or in society, often used to describe those who are not part of the elite or educated class.
  • 餔餟 - Refers to eating or living off the land.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence describes the protagonist's reaction to meeting an extraordinary person. The structure is straightforward, but the emotional tone is subtle.
  • This sentence describes the protagonist's actions the next day, preparing a meal and inviting the old man, with the intention of learning his method.
  • This sentence is a warning to the protagonist, suggesting that he should not follow the example of those who live off the land or in society.
  • This sentence expresses the old man's gratitude and his intention to repay the favor.
  • This sentence explains that the old man does not have the time or opportunity to give a gift yet.
  • This sentence is the protagonist's response, expressing his belief that the old man is deceiving him.

[214]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 披肝沥胆 - Metaphorical expression meaning to reveal one's true feelings or intentions, often used to describe someone who is being sincere and open.
  • 胥假设之词耳 - Literally means 'all assumed words,' suggesting that the previous statement was merely hypothetical or not to be taken literally.
  • 岂腹中之物，果堪持以与人哉 - Rhetorical question meaning 'How could the contents of one's stomach actually be something one could hold and give to others?' This is a playful way of dismissing a previous claim.
  • 惟自解其衣，露腹向生曰 - Literally means 'only by untying his clothes and showing his belly to the student,' indicating a physical demonstration.
  • 君试呼之，此中当有应者 - Literally means 'You try calling it, and there will be a response from within,' suggesting that the student should call out to see if something responds.
  • 生益笑而不信，坚不肯呼 - Literally means 'The student laughed even more and still did not believe, firmly refusing to call.'
  • 叟乃自拊其腹，呼曰 - Literally means 'The old man then patted his own belly and called out,' indicating a physical action followed by a verbal command.
  • 银针儿，速出见客，何作三家村儿女子态耶 - Literally means 'Silver Needle, quickly come out to meet the guest, why act like a girl from a small village?' This is a playful and somewhat dismissive command.
  • 予故厌见生人，何相逼至此 - Literally means 'I naturally dislike meeting strangers, why are you forcing me so much?' This is a response from within the belly, indicating reluctance.
  • 其音细如箫管，婉而且清 - Literally means 'The voice is as fine as a flute, soft and clear,' describing the quality of the voice.
  • 生大骇，辍笑俟之 - Literally means 'The student was greatly startled and stopped laughing, waiting for it,' indicating a change in the student's reaction.
  • 予业以汝字孙君，非蓦生者可比，妮子慎勿惧羞 - Literally means 'I have already named you Sun Jun, not a stranger, little girl, be careful not to be afraid of shame,' a reassurance to the voice within the belly.
  • 内不应 - Literally means 'There was no response inside,' indicating that the voice did not respond to the old man's reassurance.
  • 叟又促之，乃曰 - Literally means 'The old man urged again, and then said,' indicating further attempts to get a response.
  • 如此聒絮，足征阿翁老悖矣 - Literally means 'Such noise is enough to show that Old Man is old and foolish,' a playful and somewhat dismissive response.
  • 幸启半扉，予出 - Literally means 'Please open half the door, I will come out,' indicating a request to be allowed to exit the belly.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 披肝沥胆 - Common idiom in Chinese literature, often used to describe someone who is being very sincere and open.
  • 胥假设之词耳 - Phrase that suggests the previous statement was not to be taken literally, often used in philosophical or literary contexts.
  • 岂腹中之物，果堪持以与人哉 - Rhetorical question that plays on the idea of the contents of one's stomach, often used to dismiss a previous claim in a humorous way.
  • 银针儿 - Name that could be a metaphor or a playful reference to something small and delicate.
  • 三家村儿女子 - Term that refers to a girl from a small village, often used to describe someone who is simple or unsophisticated.
  • 箫管 - Type of traditional Chinese musical instrument, often used to describe a soft and clear voice.
  • 阿翁 - Term of endearment for an old man, often used in a playful or affectionate way.

Proper Nouns

  • 孙君 - Name that could be a metaphor or a specific person, but in this context, it seems to be a playful reference to the voice within the belly.
  • 阿翁 - Term of endearment for an old man, often used in a playful or affectionate way.

[215]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 生是时形如木鸡，注目呆视 - Describes the protagonist's state of shock, with '形如木鸡' and '注目呆视' forming a parallel structure.
  • 忽裂寸许，并无微血 - Describes the sudden splitting of the skin, with '忽裂寸许' and '并无微血' forming a contrasting pair.
  • 倏然异香习习，声震如裂帛 - Describes the sudden appearance of a fragrant and loud sound, with '倏然异香习习' and '声震如裂帛' forming a parallel structure.
  • 叟之迹渺然 - Indicates the disappearance of the old man, forming a clear contrast to the previous description.
  • 诧为妖异 - Indicates the protagonist's disbelief and fear, forming a clear contrast to the previous description.
  • 视其容色，又艳绝 - Describes the woman's beauty, with '视其容色' and '又艳绝' forming a descriptive sequence.
  • 不忍遽舍，乃正色叱之曰 - Describes the protagonist's change of attitude, with '不忍遽舍' and '乃正色叱之曰' forming a contrasting pair.
  • 汝诚何怪，敢以诡异惑人 - Describes the protagonist's questioning of the woman's nature, with '汝诚何怪' and '敢以诡异惑人' forming a contrasting pair.
  • 予故宋广平不为色动者，盍速退 - Describes the protagonist's self-proclaimed resistance to temptation, with '予故宋广平不为色动者' and '盍速退' forming a contrasting pair.
  • 不然，吾刃将斩矣 - Describes the protagonist's threat, forming a clear contrast to the previous description.
  • 妾实狐仙 - Describes the woman's self-identification, forming a clear contrast to the previous description.
  • 父奉上遣，将往长陵，为高皇守墓 - Describes the woman's father's mission, with '父奉上遣' and '将往长陵，为高皇守墓' forming a descriptive sequence.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 宋广平 - Refers to Song Guangping, a historical figure known for his resistance to temptation and his moral integrity.
  • 长陵 - Refers to Changling, the mausoleum of Emperor Gaozu of Han, located near Xi'an.
  • 高皇 - Refers to Emperor Gaozu of Han, the founder of the Han dynasty.
  • 水神 - Refers to the deity associated with water, often depicted as a powerful and sometimes capricious figure in Chinese mythology.
  • 狐仙 - Refers to a fox spirit, a common figure in Chinese folklore known for its ability to transform into human form and its association with both good and evil.
  • 卵翼之恩 - Refers to the kindness of nurturing, often used to describe the care and protection given to someone in need.

[216]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 欻已不见 - A set phrase meaning something disappears suddenly.
  • 惟觉有气如火，入自脐中，上达肝鬲，下行脏腑 - A metaphorical description of a sensation of heat entering the body through the navel, moving upward to the liver and diaphragm, and downward to the internal organs.
  • 须臾，汗出如蒸 - A phrase describing sweating profusely like steam, emphasizing the intensity of the experience.
  • 神思顿爽 - A phrase indicating a sudden improvement in mental or spiritual state.
  • 因而厥疾尽瘳 - A phrase indicating the complete recovery from a severe illness.
  • 重负既释，齁然熟寐 - A phrase describing a state of relaxation and deep sleep.
  • 竟罔知女之所在 - A phrase indicating a lack of knowledge about the girl's whereabouts.
  • 旦起，舟已泊岸 - A phrase describing the morning scene when the boat has arrived at the shore.
  • 僮入白：“叟已辞去，遗书一缄。” - A phrase describing the servant reporting that the old man has left and left a sealed letter.
  • 生启视，则嘱生善视其女者 - A phrase describing the young man reading the letter and finding instructions to take good care of the girl.
  • 生不得女，亦未敢深信 - A phrase indicating the young man's skepticism about the letter's contents.
  • 舍舟就舆，入城寓于其友家 - A phrase describing the young man's journey to the city and his stay at a friend's house.
  • 谈宴之间，绝无病态 - A phrase describing the young man's apparent health during social gatherings.
  • 知其疾者，咸称庆 - A phrase indicating that people who knew about his illness congratulated him on his recovery.
  • 生亦私心窃喜 - A phrase describing the young man's secret joy.
  • 语至漏下，始归寝室 - A phrase describing the late-night conversation and the young man's return to bed.
  • 生冀女复，乃令僮仆别宿 - A phrase describing the young man's hope and action to have the servants sleep elsewhere.
  • 及寝，竟杳其踪 - A phrase describing the girl's disappearance.
  • 怏怏就枕 - A phrase describing the young man's disappointment and going to bed.
  • 方转辗间，闻耳畔小语曰 - A phrase describing the young man's restlessness and hearing a soft voice.
  • 妾来相伴矣。君真铁石肠，不一动耶？ - A phrase describing the girl's appearance and her words to the young man.
  • 抚之，则腻然之玉，已在衾中 - A phrase describing the young man's touch and the girl's presence in the bed.
  • 生遂不能自持，欢然相狎 - A phrase describing the young man's loss of control and their intimate interaction.
  • 晨起谋所以匿之，女固言勿庸，果泯其迹，将寝始自来 - A phrase describing the young man's plan and the girl's reassurance.
  • 生游览已遍，归思顿萌 - A phrase describing the young man's sudden desire to return home.
  • 适怀帝践祚，诏举宾兴，遂旋返 - A phrase describing the young man's return home due to the imperial edict.
  • 女送至江畔，垂涕曰 - A phrase describing the girl's emotional farewell.
  • 父在斯，不克随郎同去矣 - A phrase describing the girl's reason for not being able to go with the young man.
  • 生亦恋恋，强之弗从，竟分袂 - A phrase describing the young man's reluctance and their final parting.
  • 明年，生下第，再诣秣陵，冀遇之，以续旧好，茫无消耗 - A phrase describing the young man's failed attempt to find the girl again.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 怀帝 - Refers to Emperor Xian of Han, who ascended the throne during the late Eastern Han period.
  • 宾兴 - An imperial edict issued to select scholars.

[217]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 外史氏曰 - A formal opening phrase used to introduce commentary or historical notes, akin to saying 'the historian says' or 'it is said that.'
  • 出入腹我 - A phrase from the *Book of Songs* (*Shijing*), which literally means 'entering and leaving my belly,' but is interpreted here as a metaphor for nurturing or raising a child.
  • 腹心之交 - A metaphor for a close, trusted friend, where 'heart and belly' symbolize deep emotional connection and trust.
  • 知心之好 - A phrase meaning 'deep understanding and affection,' often used to describe a close relationship.
  • 東床坦腹 - A reference to Wang Xizhi's story, where he famously lay on his bed during a visit to a potential father-in-law, symbolizing carefree elegance and confidence.
  • 赝殃 - A term referring to a false or illusory calamity or misfortune.
  • 回煞 - A traditional Chinese belief that the soul of a deceased person returns to the house on a specific day after death, often associated with superstitions about bad luck or misfortune.
  • 傾樽而飲 - A phrase describing drinking from a full wine vessel, often used to depict a scene of indulgence or celebration.
  • 紙窗窸窣 - Describes the sound of paper windows rustling, often used to create an eerie or unsettling atmosphere.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 外史氏曰 - The use of this phrase indicates that the text is a commentary or historical note, often found in classical Chinese literature.
  • 出入腹我 - From the *Book of Songs* (*Shijing*), a collection of ancient Chinese poetry.
  • 東床坦腹 - Refers to the famous story of Wang Xizhi, a renowned calligrapher and scholar, who lay on his bed during a visit to a potential father-in-law, demonstrating his carefree and confident nature.
  • 回煞 - A traditional Chinese belief that the soul of a deceased person returns to the house on a specific day after death, often associated with superstitions about bad luck or misfortune.
  • 傾樽而飲 - A literary device often used to describe scenes of indulgence or celebration, reflecting the cultural importance of wine in Chinese society.

Proper Nouns

  • 外史氏 - A title or pseudonym used by historians or commentators in classical Chinese literature.
  • 诗 - Refers to the *Book of Songs* (*Shijing*), a collection of ancient Chinese poetry.
  • 京都 - Refers to the capital city, often used to denote the imperial capital.
  • 子妇 - Refers to a young wife or daughter-in-law.
  • 叟 - Refers to an elderly man.
  • 女 - Refers to a woman or daughter.
  • 生 - Refers to a living person, often used to contrast with the dead.
  • 京 - Refers to the capital city.
  • 富家 - Refers to a wealthy family.

[218]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 仆不觉心胆俱落 - Hyperbolic expression meaning 'I was so frightened that my heart and courage both fell,' indicating extreme fear.
  • 悚惶间倏已逼近 - 'In a state of shock and fear, suddenly I was approached,' describing a sudden and alarming encounter.
  • 划然启扃 - 'Suddenly the door was opened,' where '划然' emphasizes the suddenness and '启扃' means 'to open the door.'
  • 骇然俱惊 - 'Both of us were terrified,' indicating a shared state of fear.
  • 颓然皆仆 - 'We both fell down,' describing a physical reaction to fear.
  • 两败俱伤 - 'Both sides suffered losses,' indicating mutual harm.
  • 平明 - 'At dawn,' referring to the early morning.
  • 莫应 - 'No response,' indicating that the servant did not answer when called.
  • 即而视之 - 'Upon seeing it,' describing an immediate action after a discovery.
  • 奄息 - 'Breathing faintly,' indicating a state of near-death.
  • 殃早绝倒于外 - 'The disaster had already fallen to the ground outside,' where '殃' refers to a calamity or misfortune.
  • 衣裳楚楚 - 'Dressed neatly,' indicating that the servant was still well-dressed.
  • 俨乎生人 - 'As if alive,' describing a lifelike appearance.
  • 发际著纸条一束 - 'A bundle of paper strips stuck to the hairline,' indicating a specific detail about the servant's appearance.
  • 实子妇之从嫁婢也 - 'It was actually the servant who accompanied the daughter-in-law in marriage,' providing context for the servant's identity.
  • 不禁愕然 - 'Could not help but be startled,' indicating a strong emotional reaction.
  • 以汤灌仆 - 'Washed the servant with hot water,' describing a medical procedure.
  • 具述所见 - 'Fully described what was seen,' indicating a detailed account.
  • 盖婢有去志 - 'It seems the servant had the intention to leave,' providing a motive.
  • 稔子妇之仪状 - 'Familiar with the daughter-in-law's appearance,' indicating prior knowledge.
  • 装束一如其在生 - 'Dressed just as she was when alive,' describing the servant's appearance.
  • 比见之 - 'Upon seeing her,' indicating an immediate reaction.
  • 不胜惊骇 - 'Could not bear the shock,' describing an overwhelming fear.
  • 遂仆于地 - 'Fell to the ground,' indicating a physical reaction.
  • 主察得其故 - 'The master understood the reason,' indicating an investigation.
  • 笑而殓之以榇 - 'Laughed and buried her in a coffin,' describing the master's reaction and action.
  • 事传都下 - 'The matter spread throughout the capital,' indicating widespread knowledge.
  • 迄今京中人语曰 - 'Until now, people in the capital say,' indicating a current belief.
  • 殃能死人，人亦能死殃 - 'Calamity can kill people, and people can also kill calamity,' a proverbial saying.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 仆 - Refers to a servant or attendant.
  • 主 - Refers to the master or owner.
  • 殃 - Refers to a calamity or misfortune, often personified in classical texts.
  • 榇 - Refers to a coffin or casket.
  • 都下 - Refers to the capital city, often used in historical contexts.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 仆不觉心胆俱落，悚惶间倏已逼近 - This sentence describes a sudden and alarming encounter, where the servant is described as being so frightened that his heart and courage both fell.
  • 划然启扃，豁然洞开 - This parallel construction emphasizes the sudden and dramatic opening of the door.
  • 骇然俱惊，颓然皆仆 - This parallel construction describes the shared physical reaction to fear.
  • 不独内者无生气，而外者亦相对死 - This sentence indicates mutual harm, where both the inside and outside parties suffered losses.
  • 平明，主人呼仆，莫应 - This sentence describes the master calling the servant, who did not respond.
  • 即而视之，仆尚奄息于中 - This sentence describes the servant still breathing faintly inside.
  • 殃早绝倒于外 - This sentence describes the calamity having already fallen to the ground outside.
  • 衣裳楚楚，俨乎生人 - This parallel construction describes the servant's neat appearance and lifelike state.
  • 发际著纸条一束，实子妇之从嫁婢也 - This sentence provides context for the servant's identity.
  • 主不禁愕然，以汤灌仆 - This sentence describes the master's reaction and action.
  • 具述所见 - This sentence indicates a detailed account of what was seen.
  • 稔子妇之仪状，装束一如其在生 - This sentence describes the servant's familiarity with the daughter-in-law's appearance and her lifelike state.
  • 比见之，不胜惊骇 - This sentence describes the immediate reaction to seeing the servant.
  • 遂仆于地 - This sentence describes the physical reaction to fear.
  • 事传都下，迄今京中人语曰 - This sentence indicates the widespread knowledge and current belief.

[220]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 罔知其死 - Literally 'not knowing his death,' used to describe the protagonist's unawareness of his own demise.
  • 顿觉身轻 - Means 'suddenly feels light,' describing the protagonist's sensation of becoming weightless.
  • 都无窒碍 - Means 'completely unobstructed,' indicating the absence of any hindrance.
  • 效列子 - Refers to emulating Liezi, a legendary figure from ancient Chinese mythology known for his ability to ride the wind.
  • 御风遨游 - Means 'riding the wind and wandering,' describing the act of traveling through the air.
  • 毫无沾濡 - Means 'not at all wet,' indicating the protagonist's dry state despite being in the midst of wind and waves.
  • 不禁大喜 - Means 'cannot help but be greatly pleased,' expressing the protagonist's joy.
  • 窃计其境必不凡 - Means 'secretly thinking that the place must be extraordinary,' indicating the protagonist's speculation about the island.
  • 转瞬即得一山 - Means 'in an instant, a mountain is seen,' describing the sudden appearance of a mountain.
  • 形如复盂 - Means 'shaped like a double basin,' describing the mountain's appearance.
  • 悬于波际 - Means 'hanging on the edge of the waves,' indicating the mountain's position over the water.
  • 其色如蜀锦 - Means 'its color is like Shu brocade,' describing the mountain's vibrant appearance.
  • 五色缤纷 - Means 'five colors in a riotous array,' indicating the mountain's colorful appearance.
  • 香气浓郁 - Means 'aroma is strong and rich,' describing the mountain's fragrance.
  • 馥馥数百里 - Means 'fragrant for hundreds of miles,' indicating the wide spread of the scent.
  • 心爱好之 - Means 'heart loves it,' expressing the protagonist's fondness for the mountain.
  • 奋身一登 - Means 'leaping up and climbing,' describing the protagonist's action of ascending the mountain.
  • 旋已舍水就陆 - Means 'soon leaves the water and goes onto land,' indicating the transition from water to land.
  • 西行里许 - Means 'walks west for several li,' describing the distance traveled.
  • 见若山口者 - Means 'sees something like a mountain pass,' describing the appearance of the mountain pass.
  • 则坦坦康庄 - Means 'then it is broad and smooth,' describing the road ahead.
  • 无复巉岩之象 - Means 'no longer has the appearance of rugged cliffs,' indicating the absence of steep and rocky terrain.
  • 山径皆落花 - Means 'the mountain path is covered with fallen flowers,' describing the landscape.
  • 约寸许 - Means 'about an inch thick,' indicating the thickness of the fallen flowers.
  • 别无隙地 - Means 'no empty space,' indicating the complete coverage of the path by flowers.
  • 踏花前进 - Means 'walking forward on the flowers,' describing the protagonist's movement.
  • 滑软如茵褥 - Means 'smooth and soft like a carpet,' describing the feel of the flowers.
  • 而香益袭鼻 - Means 'and the fragrance becomes more intense,' indicating the increasing strength of the scent.
  • 神气为之发越 - Means 'the spirit and energy are invigorated,' describing the protagonist's heightened state of being.
  • 环瞩皆茂树合抱 - Means 'looking around, all are tall trees with branches that embrace each other,' describing the surrounding vegetation.
  • 花即生于其上 - Means 'flowers grow on them,' indicating the presence of flowers on the trees.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 列子 - Refers to Liezi, a legendary figure from ancient Chinese mythology known for his ability to ride the wind and perform supernatural feats.
  • 蜀锦 - Refers to Shu brocade, a type of silk fabric from the ancient state of Shu, known for its intricate designs and vibrant colors.
  • 康庄 - Refers to a broad and smooth road, often used metaphorically to describe a smooth and easy path.
  • 巉岩 - Refers to rugged and steep cliffs, often used to describe difficult and dangerous terrain.
  • 茵褥 - Refers to a carpet or mat, often used to describe something soft and comfortable.

Proper Nouns

  • 列子 - The legendary figure Liezi.
  • 蜀锦 - The type of silk fabric Shu brocade.
  • 康庄 - The term for a broad and smooth road.
  • 巉岩 - The term for rugged and steep cliffs.
  • 茵褥 - The term for a carpet or mat.

[221]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 細玩之 - Carefully examine or study in detail.
  • 諸色俱備 - All colors are present; all varieties are included.
  • 濃淡相間 - Darker and lighter shades are intermixed.
  • 香如庾嶺之梅 - Fragrance like that of the plum blossoms on Yuling.
  • 馥郁過之 - Even more fragrant than.
  • 存於樹杪 - Remaining at the tips of the branches.
  • 低枝似墜 - Lower branches seem to be falling.
  • 繞幹如飛 - Circling the trunk as if flying.
  • 含苞欲吐 - About to bloom.
  • 四時咸有焉 - There are all four seasons.
  • 心曠神怡 - Feeling refreshed and joyful.
  • 簌簌自落若細雨然 - Falling softly like a fine rain.
  • 嬌音叱曰 - Shouted in a gentle voice.
  • 妄男子 - Rash man or reckless man.
  • 仙人所居 - Dwelling place of immortals.
  • 行樂地耶 - A place for enjoyment?
  • 通體貼以落花 - Completely covered with fallen flowers.
  • 宛如衣錦 - As if wearing brocade.
  • 小竹籃 - A small bamboo basket.
  • 貯落英 - Storing fallen blossoms.
  • 徐徐自樹後出 - Slowly emerging from behind the tree.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 庾嶺 - Yuling, a mountain range in southern China known for its plum blossoms.
  • 仙人所居 - Dwelling place of immortals, a common theme in Chinese mythology and literature.
  • 四時咸有焉 - Having all four seasons represented in a single place, a poetic and symbolic expression.

[222]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 翊起逆致揖 - Formal expression meaning 'Yi stood up and bowed in greeting.'
  • 汝一龌龊商，何福至此 - Rhetorical question meaning 'You, a lowly merchant, what good fortune has brought you here?'
  • 為予有一语，久无能对者 - Means 'I have a phrase that has long been unanswerable.'
  • 汝能，则留宿于此，且有佳处与若栖身 - Means 'If you can answer it, you may stay here and I will provide a good place for you to live.'
  • 否则，宜远飚，不容再溷仙境 - Means 'Otherwise, you should leave quickly, as you cannot remain in this sacred place.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 里甲亦与其父不相能 - 里甲 refers to a local official or headman, and 不相能 means to not get along or to be at odds. This phrase indicates a strained relationship between the local official and his father.
  • 遂具状投首于官 - 具状 means to write a formal statement or to file a complaint, and 投首 means to surrender or to confess. This phrase indicates that the local official wrote a formal statement and confessed to the authorities.
  • 熊公犹未之信 - 犹未之信 means still did not believe it. This phrase indicates that the magistrate (熊公) was skeptical of the confession.

  • 其邻证之曰 - 邻证 means to testify as a neighbor. This phrase indicates that a neighbor testified against them.
  • 若某日为若父所挞，不尝云云乎 - 若 means you, 挞 means to beat, and 不尝云云乎 means did you not say so? This phrase is a rhetorical question implying that the son had previously mentioned being beaten by his father.

This phrase indicates that the magistrate sent a messenger to neighboring towns to inquire about the existence of the peddler to determine the truth.
  • 阅数日，货郎之弟至 - 阅数日 means after several days, and 至 means to arrive. This phrase indicates that after several days, the peddler's brother arrived.
  • 衣巾登堂，则已入泮矣 - 衣巾 means dressed in formal attire, 登堂 means to enter the hall, and 入泮 means to enter the school. This phrase indicates that the brother entered the hall dressed formally, indicating he had already entered school.
  • 泣陈曰 - 泣陈 means to weep and state. This phrase indicates that the brother wept while stating his case.
  • 某年十三龄，兄即行贩不归 - 十三龄 means thirteen years old, 行贩 means to go out trading, and 不归 means not to return. This phrase indicates that the brother was thirteen years old when his brother went out trading and did not return.

  • 某又少未更事，不能远涉寻兄 - 少未更事 means young and inexperienced, and 不能远涉寻兄 means unable to travel far to find his brother. This phrase indicates that the brother was too young and inexperienced to travel far to find his brother.
  • 老母为此血泪尽枯 - 老母 means elderly mother, 为此 means because of this, and 血泪尽枯 means tears and blood dried up. This phrase indicates that the elderly mother had cried so much that her tears and blood had dried up.
  • 或存或亡，唯父师怜而鞫之 - 或存或亡 means either alive or dead, 唯 means only, 父师 means father and teacher, and 怜而鞫之 means to pity and investigate. This phrase indicates that the brother was either alive or dead, and only the father and teacher could show pity and investigate.
  • 熊公既知有其人，益严讯某父子 - 既知有其人 means having known there is such a person, 益严讯 means even more strictly interrogated, and 某父子 means the father and son. This phrase indicates that the magistrate, having known there was such a person, even more strictly interrogated the father and son.
  • 加刑者屡矣，而狡供不一，莫得尸之所在 - 加刑者屡矣 means punishment was applied repeatedly, 狡供不一 means deceitful confessions varied, and 莫得尸之所在 means no one could find the location of the body.   • 案久不结，因逮其女到官 - 案久不结 means the case remained unresolved for a long time, 因 means therefore, 逮 means to arrest, and 其女 means his daughter. This phrase indicates that because the case remained unresolved for a long time, the magistrate arrested the daughter.
  • 则嫁夫有年，亦既抱子 - 则 means then, 嫁夫有年 means married for several years, and 亦既抱子 means and already has a child. This phrase indicates that the daughter had been married for several years and already had a child.

  • 而又苦我肌肤 - Brother's complaint; '又' means and; '苦' means torment; '我肌肤' means my body.
  • 诚何忍 - Expression of emotional distress; '诚' means truly; '何忍' means how can one bear.
  • 其妹惭不言 - Sister's reaction; '惭' means ashamed; '不言' means did not speak.

  • 我可复生 - Suggestion of a dramatic turn of events; '我' means I; '可复生' means can be revived.
  • 尔妹亦免为人笑 - Implication of social consequences; '尔妹' means your sister; '亦免' means also avoid; '为人笑' means being laughed at.
  • 何哓哓如是 - Criticism of the brother's persistent complaining; '何' means why; '哓哓' means complain incessantly; '如是' means like this.
  • 其子益忿恨曰 - Son's direct speech; '其子' means his son; '益' means more; '忿恨' means resentful and angry; '曰' means said.
  • 若父女晏然 - Description of the father and daughter's demeanor; '若' means you; '父女' means father and daughter; '晏然' means calmly and unconcernedly.
  • 而官独窘我 - Son's frustration; '而' means but; '官' means official; '独' means only; '窘我' means trouble me.
  • 岂谓我独非人乎 - Rhetorical question expressing indignation; '岂' means how can it be; '谓' means say; '我独非人' means I am not a person.
  • 其妹亦温言慰之 - Sister's response; '其妹' means his sister; '亦' means also; '温言' means gentle words; '慰之' means comforted him.

  • 乃援笔判曰 - Official's decision and speech; '乃' means then; '援笔' means took up his pen; '判曰' means decided and said.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 干人 - Refers to spies or agents, a common term in classical Chinese texts.
  • 货郎 - Refers to a peddler or vendor, a common figure in classical Chinese society.
  • 王法 - Refers to the royal or imperial law, emphasizing the authority of the state.
  • 干蛊 - A term from the *Book of Changes* (Yijing) meaning to take on a difficult task or responsibility.
  • 证羊 - A metaphorical term referring to being implicated in a crime, possibly derived from the story of the scapegoat in the *Book of Rites* (Liji).

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 外史氏曰 - A traditional phrase used to introduce commentary or commentary-like statements in historical or literary texts.
  • 恒与妄男子结为亲眷 - Literally means 'always marry foolish men as relatives,' referring to men who are not suitable or trustworthy.
  • 出入闺闼 - Literally means 'enter and exit the inner chambers,' referring to men coming in and out of the private quarters of a woman’s home.
  • 墙茨不可扫 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'the wall cannot be cleaned of weeds,' symbolizing a situation that is difficult to resolve or clean up.
  • 举非丈夫所用 - Literally means 'all things not used by men,' implying that these items are not typically associated with male consumption.
  • 假此以进身 - Means 'to use this as a means to advance oneself,' suggesting that these individuals take advantage of their access to women’s homes for personal gain.
  • 闲家者所宜禁乎 - Literally means 'is it appropriate for a household to prohibit this?' A rhetorical question suggesting that households should indeed prevent such behavior.
  • 徙薪于灶 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'move the firewood away from the stove,' which implies taking preventive measures to avoid disaster.
  • 扑燎于原 - Literally means 'extinguish the flames on the plain,' which is an impossible task, suggesting that the peddler’s attempt to fix the problem was futile.
  • 败露 - Means 'to be exposed' or 'to be revealed,' implying that the peddler’s actions were discovered.
  • 身命俱捐 - Means 'both life and body were sacrificed,' indicating that the peddler lost everything.
  • 愚夫之不智 - Means 'the foolishness of the common man,' criticizing the peddler’s lack of wisdom.
  • 其亦可哀也夫 - A concluding phrase that expresses pity or sorrow, similar to 'how pitiful' or 'how sad.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 外史氏 - A term used in classical Chinese literature to refer to a historian or commentator, often used in a self-referential or meta-historical context.
  • 闺闼 - Refers to the inner chambers of a woman’s home, which were considered private and sacred spaces in traditional Chinese society.
  • 墙茨 - A metaphorical reference to weeds growing on a wall, symbolizing a situation that is difficult to resolve or clean up.
  • 卖花人与货郎 - These were common figures in traditional Chinese society, often associated with street vendors who sold goods to households. Their frequent visits to women’s homes were sometimes seen as suspicious or inappropriate.
  • 徙薪于灶 - A metaphor from *Liezi* (列子), a classic text, which advises moving firewood away from the stove to prevent a fire. This metaphor is used here to suggest that preventive measures should have been taken.
  • 扑燎于原 - Another metaphor, referring to the impossibility of extinguishing a large fire on a plain, which is used to criticize the peddler’s futile attempts to cover up his actions.

Proper Nouns

  • 外史氏 - A traditional term for a historian or commentator in classical Chinese literature.
  • 货郎 - A term for a peddler or street vendor who sold goods to households.
  • 徙薪于灶 - A metaphor from *Liezi* (列子), a classic text, which advises moving firewood away from the stove to prevent a fire.
  • 扑燎于原 - A metaphorical expression from *Liezi* (列子), which refers to the impossibility of extinguishing a large fire on a plain.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 見僧則肅然致敬，如對神明 - Formal expression meaning 'when encountering a monk, one should show profound respect as if facing a deity.'

  • 其異類如此 - Means 'such is their unusual nature,' summarizing the exceptional behavior of the monks.
  • 前藏人眾幾無孑遺 - Means 'almost all the people of the front region were left without survivors,' describing the devastating impact of the invasion.
  • 後藏人情凶惧，咸謀奔窜 - Means 'the people of the back region were filled with fear and all planned to flee,' depicting the widespread panic.
  • 逋能免乎 - Means 'how can we escape?' expressing the desperation and hopelessness of the situation.
  • 有我在，必不使汝曹罹于兵燹 - Means 'with me here, I will surely prevent you from suffering in the war,' assuring the people of protection.
  • 因率阖藏人众至一山 - Means 'therefore, he led all the people of the region to a mountain,' describing the monk's decisive action.

  • 僧尽麾之使入 - Means 'the monk drove them all inside,' showing his leadership and control.

  • 无何，敌之前锋猝至 - Means 'soon, the enemy's vanguard suddenly arrived,' describing the unexpected arrival of the attackers.
  • 见僧举嗢噱不止 - Means 'seeing the monk, they burst into laughter,' indicating the initial disbelief and mockery of the enemy.
  • 将奋往擒之 - Means 'they were about to rush forward to capture him,' showing the enemy's initial aggression.
  • 僧忽化为野豕 - Means 'the monk suddenly transformed into a wild boar,' describing the miraculous transformation.

  • 豕以一身塞之，别无余地 - Means 'the boar blocked the pass with his body, leaving no other space,' emphasizing the effectiveness of the transformation.
  • 而刚鬣乌喙，又复丑恶骇人 - Means 'with its bristly hair and black snout, it was ugly and terrifying,' describing the boar's appearance.
  • 敌众辟易 - Means 'the enemy retreated in fear,' showing the impact of the boar's appearance.
  • 适其渠酋亦至 - Means 'just then, their leader also arrived,' introducing a key figure.
  • 竞往白之，笑曰 - Means 'they all rushed to report to him, laughing and saying,' describing the interaction with the leader.
  • 此妖术也，以矢攒而射之，若屠彘然，何惧为 - Means 'this is sorcery! Shoot arrows at him, just like slaughtering a pig, what is there to fear?' expressing the leader's dismissive and arrogant attitude.
  • 众领诺，控弦数万，一时毕集 - Means 'the crowd nodded in agreement, and tens of thousands of bows were drawn, all gathering at once,' showing the overwhelming force of the enemy.
  • 弓矢未张，豕身自崖而坠 - Means 'before the bows were even drawn, the boar's body fell from the cliff,' describing the sudden and unexpected turn of events.
  • 刹那间，百千万亿举如前豕 - Means 'in an instant, countless boars appeared, all like the previous one,' emphasizing the miraculous and overwhelming nature of the transformation.
  • 蠢蠢然平川复无间隙 - Means 'they filled the plain in a chaotic and dense manner, leaving no gaps,' describing the overwhelming presence of the boars.
  • 敌众大溃，渠酋亦莫可如何，敛兵自退 - Means 'the enemy fled in disarray, and their leader could do nothing, gathering his troops and retreating,' showing the complete defeat of the enemy.
  • 僧亦不伤一骑，收法趺跏 - Means 'the monk did not harm a single rider, and he resumed his cross-legged meditation,' emphasizing the monk's non-violent and peaceful nature.
  • 其人出视之，则故胡然天帝也 - Means 'when the people emerged to see, it was the former monk, who was actually the Heavenly Emperor,' revealing the true identity of the monk.
  • 故当是时，二藏之中，惟后藏岿然独存 - Means 'at that time, in the two regions, only the back region remained standing alone,' summarizing the outcome of the conflict.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 外史氏曰 - Common phrase in classical Chinese, '外史氏' refers to the author or commentator, often used to introduce a commentary or opinion.
  • 象教 - Term referring to Buddhism, derived from the Sanskrit 'dharma,' which is often symbolized by an elephant in Buddhist iconography.
  • 巨擘 - Literally 'giant hand,' used metaphorically to mean 'the greatest or most important.'
  • 吞象 - Literally 'swallow an elephant,' used figuratively to mean 'to consume something in vast quantities.'
  • 恒河沙数 - Phrase used to describe an extremely large number, derived from the vastness of the Ganges River's sand.
  • 消虎狼之威 - Literally 'to eliminate the ferocity of tigers and wolves,' used metaphorically to mean 'to reduce the threat of fierce enemies.'
  • 保鲸鲵之众 - Literally 'to protect the multitude of whales and dolphins,' used metaphorically to mean 'to safeguard a large group of people.'
  • 大造于藏 - Literally 'greatly benefiting Tibet,' used to mean 'providing significant benefits to the region.'
  • 伟伐于佛 - Literally 'great achievements for the Buddha,' used to mean 'significant contributions to Buddhism.'
  • 警轻薄而庄严自见 - Literally 'to warn against frivolity and to reveal one's dignity,' used to mean 'to caution against superficiality and to demonstrate one's seriousness and respect.'
  • 聊斋之绩女 - Refers to the female characters in 'Liaozhai Zhiyi' (Strange Stories from a Chinese Studio), a collection of classical Chinese stories.
  • 同年而语已 - Phrase meaning 'to compare on the same level,' used here to indicate that the comparison is not appropriate.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 象教 - Refers to Buddhism, which is often symbolized by an elephant in Buddhist iconography.
  • 恒河沙数 - Refers to the vast number of grains of sand in the Ganges River, used to describe an extremely large number.
  • 虎狼 - Metaphorically refers to fierce enemies or threats.
  • 鲸鲵 - Metaphorically refers to a large group of people, often used to describe a multitude of followers or supporters.
  • 藏 - Refers to Tibet, a region historically associated with Buddhism.
  • 聊斋之绩女 - Refers to the female characters in 'Liaozhai Zhiyi,' a collection of classical Chinese stories by Pu Songling.
  • 同年而语已 - Phrase indicating that a comparison is not appropriate, suggesting that the two things being compared are fundamentally different.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '象固兽之巨擘也' uses a metaphorical expression to describe the elephant as the greatest or most important among animals.
  • The phrase '此豕直可吞象' uses a hyperbolic expression to describe the pig as capable of consuming something vast.
  • The clause '其消虎狼之威，而保鲸鲵之众' uses metaphorical language to describe the reduction of threats and the protection of a large group.
  • The clause '其有大造于藏，即有伟伐于佛矣' links the benefits to Tibet with the contributions to Buddhism.
  • The phrase '警轻薄而庄严自见' uses a metaphorical expression to describe the warning against superficiality and the demonstration of dignity.
  • The phrase '聊斋之绩女所可同年而语已' uses a comparative expression to indicate that the comparison is not appropriate.

Proper Nouns

  • 外史氏 - The author or commentator, often used to introduce a commentary or opinion.
  • 象教 - Refers to Buddhism.
  • 恒河 - Refers to the Ganges River.
  • 藏 - Refers to Tibet.
  • 聊斋之绩女 - Refers to the female characters in 'Liaozhai Zhiyi.'

  • 始亦获采 - Means 'at first he also won,' where '获采' suggests gaining some success or profit.

  • 女故知而不问 - Means 'the woman intentionally knew but did not ask,' where '故知' indicates deliberate knowledge and '不问' means not inquiring.
  • 一日，女出汲 - Means 'one day, the woman went out to draw water,' where '汲' is the act of drawing water.
  • 突遇同巷某瞥见之 - Means 'suddenly encountered someone from the same lane who saw her,' where '瞥见' means to catch a glimpse.

  • 以博得罪于势豪 - Means 'got into trouble with powerful people through gambling,' where '势豪' refers to influential or powerful individuals.
  • 方切忧惧 - Means 'was deeply worried and fearful,' where '切' emphasizes the intensity of the emotion.
  • 见女居为奇货 - Means 'saw the woman as a rare treasure,' where '奇货' metaphorically means something valuable and rare.
  • 顿思假此为释憾之计 - Means 'immediately thought of using this as a way to alleviate his regret,' where '释憾' means to relieve regret.
  • 献媚于豪 - Means 'to flatter the powerful,' where '献媚' means to curry favor.
  • 因乘间以言餂竺曰 - Means 'took the opportunity to speak to Zhu in a flattering manner,' where '乘间' means taking advantage of an opportunity and '言餂' means to speak in a flattering way.
  • 子业此欲赡两口 - Means 'you are doing this to support two people,' where '赡' means to support or provide for.
  • 势必有所不能 - Means 'there must be something you cannot do,' where '势必' indicates necessity and '有所不能' means there are limitations.

  • 始可归见里族 - Means 'only then can you return and face your fellow villagers,' where '里族' refers to one's community or fellow villagers.
  • 若仅日觅蝇头 - Means 'if you only seek small gains every day,' where '蝇头' metaphorically means small gains or minor profits.
  • 竟同株守 - Means 'you will end up like a tree standing still,' where '株守' is a metaphor for being stagnant or unproductive.
  • 不第不能归 - Means 'not only cannot you return,' where '不第' emphasizes the impossibility.
  • 归亦何颜也 - Means 'even if you return, what face will you have?' where '何颜' means what face or what dignity.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 外史氏曰 - Conventional introductory phrase used by historians or commentators to express their own opinion or commentary.
  • 青眉固功之首，而亦罪之魁 - Balanced statement that praises and criticizes the same person. '固' means 'indeed' or 'certainly,' '功之首' means 'the first in merit,' and '罪之魁' means 'the chief of sins.'
  • 非其诱竺速出，何至屡濒于险 - Rhetorical question that implies that if not for the actions of '青眉,' '竺' would not have faced repeated dangers. '诱' means 'to lure,' '速出' means 'to rush out quickly,' and '屡濒于险' means 'repeatedly close to danger.'
  • 幸而归老首丘，差可自盖 - Statement that expresses relief that '青眉' returned to a safe place, which somewhat covers his past misdeeds. '归老首丘' means 'return to one's native place in old age,' and '差可自盖' means 'somewhat able to cover oneself.'
  • 然亦竺之嗜饮嗜赌，自诒伊戚 - Statement that attributes '竺's' problems to his own habits. '嗜饮嗜赌' means 'addicted to drinking and gambling,' and '自诒伊戚' means 'bring trouble upon oneself.'
  • 岂真妇有长舌为厉之阶哉 - Rhetorical question that asks whether women with long tongues (a metaphor for gossip) are truly the cause of '竺's' troubles. '岂' means 'how' or 'how could,' '真' means 'truly,' '妇有长舌' means 'women with long tongues,' and '为厉之阶' means 'the cause of harm.'
  • 温柔乡不慕而慕醉乡，宜其有兔脱之厄 - Statement that explains why '竺' faced a dangerous situation. '温柔乡' means 'a place of comfort and ease,' '不慕而慕' means 'preferring instead,' '醉乡' means 'a place of drunkenness,' and '宜其有兔脱之厄' means 'it is appropriate that he faced a dangerous situation.'
  • 恩爱海不贪而贪苦海，宜其有鼠窜之危 - Statement that further explains '竺's' misfortune. '恩爱海' means 'the sea of love,' '不贪而贪' means 'not seeking but instead seeking,' '苦海' means 'the sea of suffering,' and '宜其有鼠窜之危' means 'it is appropriate that he faced a dangerous situation.'
  • 故罪不可不专责之青眉，究亦不能末减于竺皮 - Concluding statement that assigns blame to '青眉' but also acknowledges '竺's' responsibility. '故' means 'therefore,' '罪不可不专责之青眉' means 'the blame cannot be exclusively assigned to Qingmei,' '究亦不能末减于竺皮' means 'ultimately, it cannot be completely mitigated for Zhubi.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '既而曰："后晤犹远，君能伴妾一往，庶识其处，为异日觅缘之地。"' is a direct speech block, where the speaker expresses a request and a hope.
  • The sentence '宗亦欲觇其异，欣然从之。' connects the narrator's desire to see something unusual with his willing compliance.
  • The sentence '出门即有油壁小车，驾以良马，使者凡两人，面目狞恶，而侍女颇恭。' describes the scene outside the house, with a focus on the carriage, the messengers, and the maids.
  • The sentence '女指宗语之曰："若人即我之夫子，将随往视我门闾，幸勿阻。"' is a direct speech block, where the woman explains the narrator's role and the purpose of the journey.
  • The phrase '使者唯唯。' indicates the messengers' agreement or assent.
  • The sentence '宗遂与女共乘，车驰如风。' describes the narrator and the woman riding together in the carriage, which moves quickly.
  • The sentence '途次似有城郭，皆不及注目，女在车中谓宗曰："凡人转生，必昧前因，至死乃复悟，妾此行当亦如之。他日相逢，恐不免以少长为憾，郎十八旧作，君尚能记忆否？"' describes the journey and the woman's reflections on rebirth and memory.
  • The sentence '宗答曰："虽得其概，未详其词。"' is a direct speech block, where the narrator responds to the woman's question.

  • 以孝廉来宰是邑 - 以孝廉: being selected as a孝廉 (filial and upright official), a title given to candidates recommended by local officials for the imperial examination. 宰是邑: to govern this county.
  • 心窃讶焉 - 心窃: secretly in one's heart,讶: to be surprised. Together, it conveys a sense of private astonishment.
  • 询诸绅衿 - 询: to inquire,绅衿: local gentry or educated elite.
  • 力白其孤洁 - 力白: to strongly defend,孤洁: pure and uncorrupted. This phrase indicates that the gentry strongly defended the nun's purity.
  • 汪究惝恍不定 - 汪究: Master Wang pondered,惝恍: uncertain or confused,不定: unresolved. This phrase suggests that Master Wang was still uncertain.
  • 遣干人密侦于左近 - 遣: to send,干人: subordinate or agent,密侦: to secretly investigate. This phrase indicates that Master Wang sent someone to secretly investigate.
  • 旬余 - 旬: a period of ten days,余: more than. Together, it means more than ten days.
  • 土工许二 - 土工: laborer or worker,许二: proper name, Xu Er.
  • 嫚骂不绝 - 嫚骂: to abuse or insult,不绝: continuously. This phrase indicates that Xu Er was continuously insulting someone.
  • 语语皆侵静定 - 语语: every word,侵: to insult. This phrase suggests that every word was an insult directed at the nun.
  • 干人以为疑 - 干人: the agent,以为疑: to find suspicious. This phrase indicates that the agent found the situation suspicious.
  • 翌日 - 翌日: the next day.
  • 矫宰命遍召土木之工 - 矫: to pretend or forge,宰命: command of the magistrate,遍召: to summon all,土木之工: workers in construction. This phrase indicates that the agent pretended to summon all construction workers.
  • 独阴厚许 - 独: only,阴厚: secretly favored,许: Xu Er. This phrase suggests that Xu Er was secretly favored.
  • 许心感之 - 许: Xu Er,心感: felt grateful,之: the agent. This phrase indicates that Xu Er felt grateful.
  • 若某日于某庵前 - 若: if,某日: on a certain day,某庵前: in front of a certain temple. This phrase is a hypothetical statement.
  • 何暴怒乃尔 - 何: why,暴怒: to rage,乃尔: to this extent. This phrase asks why Xu Er was so angry.
  • 淫婢子许我重赇 - 淫婢子: derogatory term for the nun,许我: promised me,重赇: large bribe. This phrase indicates that the nun promised Xu Er a large bribe.
  • 每日五贯 - 每日: every day,五贯: sum of money. This phrase specifies the amount of the bribe.
  • 今竟悍不肯与 - 今: now,竟: finally,悍: stubborn,不肯与: refuses to give. This phrase indicates that the nun was now stubborn and refused to give the bribe.
  • 故小辱之 - 故: therefore,小辱: slight insult,之: the nun. This phrase indicates that Xu Er insulted the nun slightly.
  • 干人佯讶曰 - 干人: the agent,佯讶: pretended to be surprised,曰: said. This phrase indicates that the agent pretended to be surprised and said something.
  • 静师素有清德 - 静师: the nun,素有: has always had,清德: pure virtue. This phrase indicates that the nun has always been virtuous.
  • 以何事而赂汝 - 以何事: for what reason,赂汝: to bribe you. This phrase asks why the nun would bribe Xu Er.
  • 得毋诬乎 - 得毋: is it not,诬: false. This phrase suggests that the agent is questioning the truthfulness of Xu Er's statement.
  • 许怒曰 - 许: Xu Er,怒曰: angrily said. This phrase indicates that Xu Er angrily responded.
  • 秃娼何清何德 - 秃娼: derogatory term for the nun,何清何德: what purity and virtue. This phrase is a sarcastic remark by Xu Er.
  • 惟予深悉之 - 惟: only,予: I,深悉之: know it well. This phrase indicates that Xu Er knows the nun well.
  • 近寺僧，皆其所欢也 - 近寺僧: monks near the temple,皆其所欢也: are all her lovers. This phrase suggests that the nun had relationships with the monks.
  • 虑为人所踪迹 - 虑: fear,为人所: by others,踪迹: to track or follow. This phrase indicates that the nun feared being followed.
  • 乃以五十缗浼予为地道 - 乃: then,以: with,五十缗: sum of money,浼: to ask,为地道: to dig a tunnel. This phrase indicates that the nun asked Xu Er to dig a tunnel.
  • 自某家坟侧直至庵中 - 自: from,某家坟侧: side of a certain family's tomb,直至庵中: to the temple. This phrase describes the route of the tunnel.
  • 凡四十余丈 - 凡: total,四十余丈: more than forty zhang (a unit of length). This phrase specifies the length of the tunnel.
  • 号为方便门 - 号: called,方便门: convenient gate. This phrase indicates that the tunnel was called a convenient gate.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 孝廉 - A title given to candidates recommended by local officials for the imperial examination, emphasizing filial piety and integrity.
  • 绅衿 - Refers to the local gentry or educated elite, who often played a significant role in local governance.
  • 僧 - Refers to monks or Buddhist monks.
  • 尼 - Refers to nuns or Buddhist nuns.
  • 淫婢子 - A derogatory term for a nun, suggesting immorality or promiscuity.
  • 秃娼 - Another derogatory term for a nun, implying that she is a prostitute.
  • 五十缗 - A sum of money, 缗 refers to a unit of currency.
  • 地道 - A tunnel or underground passage, often used for secret communication or escape routes.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 以孝廉来宰是邑 - The sentence structure is clear, but the phrase 以孝廉 could be interpreted as either as a孝廉 or by virtue of being a孝廉.
  • 心窃讶焉 - The phrase 心窃 is a bit ambiguous, as it could be interpreted as secretly in one's heart or quietly in one's heart.
  • 询诸绅衿 - The phrase 询诸 is a bit ambiguous, as it could be interpreted as inquired of or consulted with.
  • 力白其孤洁 - The phrase 力白 is a bit ambiguous, as it could be interpreted as strongly defended or vigorously defended.
  • 汪究惝恍不定 - The phrase 汪究 is a bit ambiguous, as it could be interpreted as Master Wang pondered or Master Wang deliberated.
  • 遣干人密侦于左近 - The phrase 遣干人 is a bit ambiguous, as it could be interpreted as sent a subordinate or dispatched an agent.
  • 旬余 - The phrase 旬余 is a bit ambiguous, as it could be interpreted as more than ten days or a period of ten days and some more.

  • 乃于是月之望，五鼓出邑，诣法祥寺行香 - Describes an event occurring on the night of the full moon, with the person leaving the town at the fifth drumbeat to visit a temple for incense offerings.
  • 既至，僧始知，亟弃所乐出迎宰官 - Describes the monks realizing the arrival and quickly abandoning their entertainment to welcome the official.
  • 汪绐主僧曰 - Describes Wang deceiving the head monk.
  • 汝寺之众约几何？可悉告予，予将普施舍 - A question asking about the number of monks in the temple, with a promise to provide widespread charity.
  • 主僧仓卒以数对，中实缺一二人 - Describes the head monk hastily providing a number, which is actually short by one or two people.
  • 汪指数之，阳怒曰 - Describes Wang pointing out the discrepancy and pretending to be angry.
  • 何竟诳予？是必藐我之职，有高枕不出者 - Rhetorical question expressing suspicion and the belief that someone is disrespecting Wang's authority by staying in bed.
  • 命役遍索于各房，得诸尼于卧榻，絷之以出，皆裸如也 - Describes Wang ordering a search of the rooms, finding the nuns in their beds, and binding them before taking them out, all of whom are naked.
  • 汪笑谓主僧曰 - Describes Wang smiling and speaking to the head monk.
  • 阻汝佳会，颇杀风景 - Expresses regret at interrupting a pleasant gathering, suggesting that it spoils the mood.
  • 然世尊则攒眉久矣 - Suggests that the Buddha has been frowning for a long time, implying disappointment or disapproval.
  • 主僧顿首至流血 - Describes the head monk bowing so deeply that he bleeds.
  • 汪命尽拘之，而尼中独不见静定 - Describes Wang ordering the arrest of everyone, but noting that one nun, named Jingding, is missing.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence contrasts universal human emotion with the monk's specific devotion.
  • The rhetorical question contrasts universal human desire with the loss to luxury.
  • The sentence links the monk's sighing with the lamentation of a lost goal.
  • The sentence links the monk's lingering with a secret pact or division.
  • The sentence contrasts the grievance of the gods with the power of the Vajras.
  • The sentence links the illumination of wisdom with secretive communication.
  • The sentence links the refined environment with the state of deep contemplation.
  • The sentence links the monk's robe with the marks of passion or desire.
  • The sentence links the search for something elusive with the giving up of worldly pleasures.
  • The sentence links entanglement with something hanging or resting.
  • The sentence links the state of depletion with the dispersion of precious things.
  • The sentence links the grudge that should be resolved with a specific position.
  • The sentence links profound and enduring emotion with the preservation of purity.
  • The sentence links the struggle to find a solution with the elimination of deep-seated troubles.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 無常鬼 - A ghost associated with death, leading souls to the underworld; common in Chinese folklore.
  • 循表道之樹 - Walking along a road marked by trees; '循' means to follow, '表道' refers to a marked path, '之樹' means 'of the tree'.
  • 徒步而疾行 - Walking quickly on foot; '徒步' means on foot, '疾行' means to walk quickly.
  • 矗立 - Standing erect or towering; often used to describe something standing tall and straight.
  • 搖搖动荡 - Swaying or moving back and forth; describes unstable or fluctuating conditions.
  • 仿佛云影徘徊 - Looks like a cloud shadow lingering or hovering; '仿佛' means as if, '云影' means cloud shadow, '徘徊' means to linger.
  • 跬步越之 - Taking steps that surpass or exceed the usual distance; '跬步' means a step, '越之' means to exceed.
  • 似无余地 - Seems to have no space left; '似' means as if, '无余地' means no room left.
  • 大骇 - Greatly frightened or terrified.
  • 惧不敢前 - So afraid that one cannot move forward; '惧' means fear, '不敢前' means dare not go forward.
  • 俟其去而后行 - Waiting for something to leave before continuing; '俟' means to wait, '其去' means for it to leave, '而后行' means and then proceed.
  • 踱如故 - Pacing as it was before; '踱' means to pace, '如故' means as before.
  • 两袂綦宽 - The sleeves being very wide; '两袂' means two sleeves, '綦宽' means very wide.
  • 累累然有物蠕动 - Something wriggling continuously; '累累然' means in a continuous manner, '有物蠕动' means something is wriggling.
  • 渐作鬼声 - Gradually making ghostly sounds; '渐' means gradually, '作鬼声' means making ghostly sounds.
  • 反身就故道 - Turning around and returning to the original path; '反身' means to turn around, '就故道' means to return to the original path.
  • 另觅曲径而归 - Looking for another winding path and returning; '另觅' means to look for another, '曲径' means a winding path, '而归' means and return.
  • 竟亦无恙 - Finally returning without any harm; '竟' means finally, '亦无恙' means also without harm.
  • 或谓为无常鬼 - Some say it is the ghost of the Death Messenger; '或' means some say, '谓' means say, '为无常鬼' means it is the ghost of the Death Messenger.
  • 似亦近是 - It is close to being right; '似' means as if, '亦近是' means also close to being right.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 蘇連浮三爵 - Classical expression meaning 'Su drank three cups of wine in succession.' '浮' (floating) is used metaphorically for drinking, and '爵' (爵) refers to a type of wine cup.
  • 甘露醍醐 - Metaphorical phrase meaning 'sweet dew and pure milk,' used to describe something extremely pleasant or delightful.
  • 煩燥頓解 - Means 'the irritation was suddenly relieved,' where '煩燥' refers to restlessness or agitation, and '頓解' means 'suddenly resolved.'
  • 益大悅 - Means 'even more delighted,' where '益' means 'even more' and '大悅' means 'greatly pleased.'
  • 俄而 - A temporal adverb meaning 'soon after' or 'in a short while.'
  • 钲鸣若雷，铙喧如沸 - Describes the sound of drums and cymbals, with '钲' (zheng) referring to a large gong, '铙' (nao) to a cymbal, and '若雷' and '如沸' used to emphasize the loudness.
  • 优人扮鬼十数 - Means 'actors dressed as ghosts, numbering in the dozens,' where '优人' (you ren) refers to performers or actors.

  • 手足割裂，脏腑狼藉 - Describes the scene of dismemberment, with '手足割裂' meaning 'hands and feet cut apart' and '脏腑狼藉' meaning 'organs scattered in disorder.'
  • 血雨腥风 - Metaphorical phrase meaning 'blood raining and stench of wind,' used to describe a horrific scene.
  • 扑鼻惨目 - Means 'stinking and horrifying to the eyes,' where '扑鼻' (pu bi) means 'stinking to the nose' and '惨目' (can mu) means 'horrifying to the eyes.'
  • 苏素未睹此 - Means 'Su had never seen this before,' where '素' means 'never' and '未睹此' means 'had not seen this.'
  • 不觉大惊 - Means 'he was suddenly very frightened,' where '不觉' (bu jue) means 'suddenly' and '大惊' means 'very frightened.'
  • 以袂自障其面 - Means 'he covered his face with his sleeve,' where '袂' (mei) refers to the sleeve of a robe.
  • 汗如雨下 - Metaphorical phrase meaning 'sweat poured down like rain,' used to describe extreme fear or exertion.
  • 耳边惟闻嘶痛之声 - Means 'he only heard the sound of screams and cries,' where '嘶痛' (si tong) means 'screams and cries.'
  • 股傈欲走 - Means 'his legs trembled and he wanted to run,' where '股傈' (gu li) means 'trembling legs' and '欲走' means 'wanted to run.'
  • 良久 - Means 'after a long time,' where '良' (liang) means 'long' and '久' (jiu) means 'time.'
  • 张目视之 - Means 'he opened his eyes and looked,' where '张目' (zhang mu) means 'opened his eyes' and '视之' (shi zhi) means 'looked at it.'
  • 寄身廊庑 - Means 'he found himself in a corridor,' where '寄身' (ji shen) means 'found himself' and '廊庑' (lang wu) refers to a corridor or hallway.
  • 霍然愈矣 - Means 'he suddenly recovered,' where '霍然' (huo ran) means 'suddenly' and '愈' (yu) means 'recovered.'
  • 始知其梦 - Means 'he finally realized it was a dream,' where '始知' (shi zhi) means 'finally realized' and '其梦' (qi meng) means 'his dream.'
  • 且叹其奇 - Means 'and he marveled at its strangeness,' where '且' (qie) means 'and' and '叹其奇' (tan qi qi) means 'marveled at its strangeness.'
  • 因匍匐往寻道众 - Means 'he then crawled to find the Daoists,' where '匍匐' (pu fu) means 'crawled' and '寻道众' (xun dao zhong) means 'found the Daoists.'
  • 饮以粥汤 - Means 'he gave him porridge and soup,' where '饮以' (yin yi) means 'gave him' and '粥汤' (zhu tang) means 'porridge and soup.'
  • 不旬日，而复初 - Means 'after a few days, he returned to his original state,' where '不旬日' (bu xun ri) means 'after a few days' and '而复初' (er fu chu) means 'returned to his original state.'
  • 后过岳庙 - Means 'later, he passed by the Yue Temple,' where '后' (hou) means 'later' and '过岳庙' (guo yue miao) means 'passed by the Yue Temple.'

  • 酷似梦中东道主 - Means 'they were extremely similar to the host in the dream,' where '酷似' (ku si) means 'extremely similar' and '梦中东道主' (meng zhong dong dao zhu) means 'the host in the dream.'
  • 但不知所延者为何客耳 - Means 'but he did not know who the guest was,' where '但' (dan) means 'but,' '不知' (bu zhi) means 'did not know,' and '所延者为何客耳' (suo yan zhe wei he ke er) means 'who the guest was.'
  • 又半年，始归故乡 - Means 'after another six months, he finally returned to his hometown,' where '又半年' (you ban nian) means 'after another six months' and '始归故乡' (shi gui gu xiang) means 'finally returned to his hometown.'
  • 病疫者每乐闻其说 - Means 'those who were sick with plague often enjoyed hearing this story,' where '病疫者' (bing yi zhe) means 'those who were sick with plague,' '每' (mei) means 'often,' and '乐闻其说' (le wen qi shuo) means 'enjoyed hearing this story.'
  • 比诸子章髑髅云 - Means 'it is comparable to the story of Zixia and the skull,' where '比诸' (bi zhu) means 'comparable to,' '子章' (zi zhang) refers to Zixia, a disciple of Confucius, and '髑髅云' (du lou yun) means 'the story of the skull.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '苏连浮三爵，俨如甘露醍醐，烦燥顿解，益大悦' describes Su's experience of drinking wine and feeling refreshed. The structure is straightforward, but the metaphorical language requires careful handling.
  • The sentence '俄而钲鸣若雷，铙喧如沸，优人扮鬼十数，当场而支解之' describes a sudden and chaotic scene involving performers and dismemberment. The parallel structure and vivid imagery require clear translation.

  • 春光泄矣，可若何 - The spring has been revealed, what can be done now? '春光泄矣' metaphorically refers to the exposure of a secret or private matter.

  • 渠翁性乖戾，乡邻有小过、辄龂龂而道之 - Her husband is ill-tempered, and he always argues with neighbors over minor faults. '乖戾' means 'ill-tempered' or 'irascible,' and '龂龂' describes persistent arguing.
  • 况姻娅而有是事乎 - How much more so when it comes to family matters? '姻娅' refers to relatives by marriage.
  • 予父母必置予于死地矣 - My parents will surely kill me. '置予于死地' is a strong expression meaning 'to put someone to death.'
  • 齐姓，故无赖，非乡中人，家于邑城，甚饶裕 - He is surnamed Qi, a rascal, not from the village, lives in the county town, and is very wealthy. '无赖' refers to a rascal or a man without a fixed occupation.
  • 缘视佃人刈获，始来此 - Because he was watching the tenant farmers harvest, he came here. '刈获' means 'harvesting.'
  • 见姑与女偕，好丑攸分，恍辛夷之与桃李 - Seeing the mother-in-law and daughter together, the difference in beauty was clear, like the contrast between magnolia and peach blossoms. '好丑攸分' means 'clear distinction between good and bad,' and '恍辛夷之与桃李' is a metaphor for a stark contrast.
  • 心大动 - His heart was greatly stirred. '大动' means 'greatly stirred' or 'excited.'
  • 既而闻聂父子之陗厉 - Later, he heard about the severity of the father and son. '陗厉' means 'severe' or 'strict.'
  • 女又端重不佻 - The daughter is dignified and not frivolous. '端重' means 'dignified,' and '不佻' means 'not frivolous.'
  • 遂假途于姑 - He then took a detour through the mother-in-law. '假途' means 'taking a detour' or 'using someone as a means to an end.'
  • 原以图女，非止得陇而始望蜀者 - He originally intended to pursue the daughter, not someone who is content with what he has. '得陇而始望蜀' is an idiom meaning 'to be content with what one has.'
  • 闻言大喜，乃谓姑曰 - Hearing this, he was greatly pleased and said to the mother-in-law. '闻言' means 'upon hearing.'
  • 若惧乎？是在人谋 - Are you afraid? It depends on human planning. '人谋' means 'human planning' or 'strategy.'
  • 倘以计兼致之，则弭其口不难矣 - If we plan to bring both of them together, it won't be hard to silence them. '弭其口' means 'to silence them.'
  • 姑以首示之意，徐曰 - The mother-in-law nodded to indicate her agreement and said slowly. '以首示之意' means 'nodded to indicate her agreement.'
  • 是诚不能。渠夫年正壮，琴瑟綦调，非如我无人操缦者 - I truly cannot. Her husband is in his prime, and their relationship is very harmonious, unlike me who is without a partner. '琴瑟綦调' means 'very harmonious,' and '无人操缦者' means 'without a partner.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 人物之形形色色、怪怪奇奇繁且赜矣 - Describes the diversity and complexity of people and things in the world, with '形形色色' meaning 'various and diverse' and '怪怪奇奇' meaning 'strange and peculiar'.
  • 目非瞆而似瞆者，动谓以目所亲见者为真 - Describes people who, though not blind, act as if they are, often believing what they see with their own eyes is true.
  • 耳非聋而似聋者，动谓以耳所亲闻者为实 - Similarly, this describes people who, though not deaf, act as if they are, often believing what they hear with their own ears is true.
  • 浸假而纷呈于吾目，殆熟视若无睹耶 - '浸假' means 'if' or 'suppose,' and '纷呈' means 'presented in abundance.' The phrase suggests that even if everything were presented to one's eyes, one might still ignore it due to familiarity.
  • 浸假而交集于吾耳，殆习听若不察耶 - Similar to the previous phrase, this suggests that even if everything were heard, one might still fail to understand it due to habit.
  • 庸耳俗目之交，殆不足语以天地之大，四海九州之广，形形色色、怪怪奇奇之繁且赜邪 - This phrase criticizes the common people's inability to comprehend the vastness and complexity of the world.
  • 其思入窈冥者，可断以理之所必无也 - '窈冥' means 'deep and dark,' and '理之所必无' means 'what reason dictates must not exist.' This phrase criticizes stories that are too fantastical.
  • 其言归讽谕者，可信为情之所或有也 - '归讽谕' means 'to return to admonition,' and '情之所或有' means 'what emotion or reality might have.' This phrase praises stories that have a moral or practical lesson.
  • 吾不知见之者咋舌凡几辈耶 - '咋舌' means 'to be shocked or amazed.'

  • 又不知广大繁赜者，将尽泯没于咋舌蹙额之流耶 - '泯没' means 'to be lost or forgotten,' and '咋舌蹙额之流' refers to those who are shocked or displeased.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sequence '迨启其囊，明珠千粒，价固万金不啻，始信邵为遇仙' describes the discovery of pearls and the realization of an extraordinary event.
  • The sequence '比至家，未卸装，即询邵生近况，果以事下狱，至夜卒' describes the rapid succession of events upon arriving home.
  • The sequence '且述其异，死不于监而于门，趺坐如生，身旁有书一椷，语甚怪' describes the strange circumstances of the man's death and the mysterious letter.
  • The clause '有见之者，窃言其略曰' introduces the content of the letter as described by witnesses.
  • The long sequence '今伤差之罪，予已论抵。杀人之罪，谁职其咎？夫虺蜴入室，犹思碎之，况盗乎？盗移盗尸而证杀盗者为盗，官竟目之为盗，非故出故入乎？欲求真盗，则首盗者是' questions the fairness of the legal judgment and identifies the true culprit.
  • The clause '末钤巨印，则故衡山大帝篆文也' identifies the source of the letter.
  • The sequence '官惊秘其事，而捕群盗，遂究移尸之状，尽置于法' describes the official's reaction and the subsequent legal proceedings.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 因自奇其计 - Because he thought his plan was extraordinary. The word '奇' (qí) can mean 'extraordinary' or 'strange,' and here it is used to convey that the speaker found his plan remarkable.
  • 遂以授之于子 - So he entrusted it to his son. The word '遂' (suì) indicates a result or consequence, and '授之' (shòu zhī) means 'to entrust' or 'to give.'
  • 果能免患 - And indeed it could save him from trouble. The word '果' (guǒ) means 'indeed' or 'really,' and '免患' (miǎn huàn) means 'to avoid trouble.'
  • 然予有女在，不遂远游 - But I have a daughter, so I cannot go on a long journey. The word '然' (rán) is a conjunction meaning 'but,' and '不遂' (bù suì) means 'cannot' or 'will not.'
  • 意将委之于人，非子不可 - I intend to entrust it to someone, but only you are suitable. The word '意将' (yì jiāng) means 'intend to,' and '非子不可' (fēi zǐ bù kě) means 'only you can.'
  • 倘得子之金诺 - If I can get your golden promise. The word '倘' (tǎng) means 'if,' and '金诺' (jīn nuò) is a metaphor for a solemn and reliable promise.
  • 则我可脱然无累 - Then I can be free from all burdens. The word '脱然' (tuō rán) means 'free from' or 'unburdened,' and '无累' (wú lèi) means 'without burden.'
  • 往朝南海，成其正果矣 - Go to the South Sea and achieve your true goal. The phrase '成其正果' (chéng qí zhèng guǒ) is a Buddhist term meaning 'to achieve enlightenment' or 'to fulfill one's true purpose.'
  • 喜出望外 - Delighted beyond expectation.
  • 亟以婿礼拜见 - Hurriedly paid respects as a son-in-law. The word '亟' (jí) means 'hurriedly,' and '婿礼拜见' (xù lǐ bài jiàn) means 'paid respects as a son-in-law.'
  • 媪大悦，起曰 - The old lady was very pleased and stood up to say. The word '媪' (ǎo) refers to an elderly woman, and '大悦' (dà yuè) means 'very pleased.'
  • 今夕颇良，可使迨吉者来事夫子 - Today is auspicious, so let the matchmaker come to arrange the marriage. The phrase '迨吉者' (dài jí zhě) refers to a matchmaker or a person who arranges auspicious events.
  • 遂匆匆亟去 - So she hurriedly left. The word '匆匆' (cōng cōng) means 'hurriedly,' and '亟去' (jí qù) means 'left quickly.'
  • 未几，侍婢数人捧丽人自外而入 - Not long after, several maidservants brought a beautiful woman from outside. The phrase '未几' (wèi jǐ) means 'not long after,' and '侍婢' (shì bì) refers to maidservants.
  • 装饰如贵家，绝非向之落魄者 - She was dressed like a noblewoman, far different from the previous poor woman. The phrase '装饰如贵家' (zhuāng shì rú guì jiā) means 'dressed like a noblewoman,' and '绝非向之落魄者' (jué fēi xiàng zhī luò pò zhě) means 'far different from the previous poor woman.'
  • 比睨之，果即前者少艾 - When I looked at her closely, she was indeed the young and beautiful woman from before. The phrase '比睨之' (bǐ nì zhī) means 'looked at her closely,' and '果即前者少艾' (guǒ jí qián zhě shào yì) means 'indeed the young and beautiful woman from before.'
  • 益慰所怀 - Which greatly comforted my heart. The phrase '益慰所怀' (yì wèi suǒ huái) means 'greatly comforted my heart.'
  • 于是欢欣与语，女终腼腆不答 - So we happily conversed, but the girl remained shy and did not respond. The phrase '欢欣与语' (huān xīn yǔ yǔ) means 'happily conversed,' and '女终腼腆不答' (nǚ zhōng miǎn tiǎn bù dá) means 'the girl remained shy and did not respond.'
  • 其母又来促之寝 - Her mother then came and urged her to sleep. The phrase '促之寝' (cù zhī qǐn) means 'urged her to sleep.'
  • 乃屏烛下帷，解衣同梦 - So they extinguished the candles, drew the curtains, and undressed to sleep together. The phrase '屏烛下帷' (píng zhú xià wéi) means 'extinguished the candles and drew the curtains,' and '解衣同梦' (jiě yī tóng mèng) means 'undressed to sleep together.'
  • 海棠湿雨，杜鹃啼春 - These are poetic phrases describing the beauty of nature. '海棠湿雨' (hǎi táng shī yǔ) means 'the rain-soaked hawthorn blossoms,' and '杜鹃啼春' (dù juān tí chūn) means 'the cuckoo bird sings in spring.'
  • 竟不知夜之几何矣 - They did not know how late it had become. The phrase '竟不知夜之几何矣' (jìng bù zhī yè zhī jǐ hé yǐ) means 'did not know how late it had become.'
  • 昧爽，媪即至 - At dawn, the old lady arrived. The phrase '昧爽' (mèi shuǎng) means 'at dawn.'
  • 赠婿以赤金二笏曰 - She gave her son-in-law two pieces of red gold and said. The phrase '赤金二笏' (chì jīn èr hù) means 'two pieces of red gold,' and '曰' (yuē) means 'said.'
  • 多与恐汝不任，然已可吃著此生矣 - If I give you too much, you might not be able to handle it, but this should be enough for your lifetime. The phrase '多与恐汝不任' (duō yǔ kǒng rǔ bù rèn) means 'if I give you too much, you might not be able to handle it,' and '然已可吃著此生矣' (rán yǐ kě chī zhe cǐ shēng yǐ) means 'but this should be enough for your lifetime.'
  • 径留女自返，女亦不甚流连 - She left the girl with him and returned herself, and the girl did not linger. The phrase '径留女自返' (jìng liú nǚ zì fǎn) means 'left the girl with him and returned herself,' and '女亦不甚流连' (nǚ yì bù shèn liú lián) means 'the girl did not linger.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 南海 - Refers to the South Sea, which is often associated with the sea god and the realm of immortals in Chinese mythology.
  • 正果 - A Buddhist term meaning 'to achieve enlightenment' or 'to fulfill one's true purpose.'
  • 婿礼拜见 - Refers to the traditional Chinese practice of a son-in-law paying respects to his father-in-law and mother-in-law.
  • 迨吉者 - Refers to a matchmaker or a person who arranges auspicious events in traditional Chinese culture.
  • 贵家 - Refers to a noble or wealthy family in traditional Chinese society.
  • 海棠湿雨，杜鹃啼春 - These are poetic phrases describing the beauty of nature, often used in classical Chinese literature to evoke a sense of beauty and tranquility.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 妝讫 - 妝 means to dress up or adorn oneself, and 讒 means to finish or complete. Together, it means 'after finishing dressing up.'
  • 辄与十三对坐 - 辄 means to immediately or without hesitation, 与 means to be with, and 对坐 means to sit facing each other. Together, it means 'immediately sat facing each other.'
  • 他人往来皆莫之见 - 他人往来 means 'others coming and going,' 皆 means 'all,' and 莫之见 means 'did not see it.' Together, it means 'others coming and going did not see it.'
  • 倏然不见其妻 - 倏然 means suddenly or instantly, and 不见其妻 means 'did not see his wife.' Together, it means 'suddenly did not see his wife.'
  • 怪而问之 - 怪 means to find strange or unusual, and 问之 means to ask about it. Together, it means 'found it strange and asked about it.'
  • 笑曰 - 笑 means to laugh, and 曰 means to say. Together, it means 'laughed and said.'
  • 妾实神人 - 妾 is a modest term for a woman, 实 means actually, and 神人 means a divine being. Together, it means 'I am actually a divine being.'
  • 彼龌龊商人乌能见予哉 - 彼 means he, 龌龊 means dirty or base, 商人 means merchant, 乌能 means how could, 见予 means see me, and 哉 is an interrogative particle. Together, it means 'how could that dirty merchant see me?'
  • 将至乡里 - 将至 means about to arrive, and 乡里 means hometown or village. Together, it means 'about to arrive at the village.'
  • 女与十三谋 - 女 means the woman, 与 means to, 十三 is a person's name, and 谋 means to plan or scheme. Together, it means 'the woman planned with Thirteen.'
  • 别舍于外 - 别舍 means to separate or isolate, and 于外 means outside. Together, it means 'separated herself outside.'
  • 驰告于父 - 驰告 means to rush and inform, and 于父 means to the father. Together, it means 'rushed to inform the father.'
  • 舟中皆谓儿智 - 舟中 means on the boat, 皆 means all, 谓 means to call or consider, and 儿智 means the son is intelligent. Together, it means 'all on the boat considered the son intelligent.'
  • 有客愿以女嫁儿 - 有客 means there is a guest, 愿 means wishes, and 以女嫁儿 means to marry his daughter to the son. Together, it means 'there is a guest who wishes to marry his daughter to the son.'
  • 兼赠重金 - 兼 means also, 赠 means to give as a gift, and 重金 means a large sum of money. Together, it means 'also gives a large sum of money.'
  • 彼适有远行 - 彼 means he, 适 means just now, and 有远行 means has a long journey. Together, it means 'he just now has a long journey.'
  • 不获亲来讲姻谊 - 不获 means unable to, 亲来 means personally come, and 讲姻谊 means discuss marital relations. Together, it means 'unable to personally discuss marital relations.'
  • 先遣其女于归矣 - 先遣 means first send, 其女 means his daughter, 于归 means return to her husband's home, and 矣 is a particle indicating completion. Together, it means 'first sent his daughter to return to her husband's home.'
  • 敢请父命 - 敢 means dare, 请 means to ask, and 父命 means father's command. Together, it means 'dare to ask the father's command.'
  • 崔父闻子言念一事 - 崔父 means Cui's father, 闻 means to hear, 子言 means the son's words, and 念一事 means think of one thing. Together, it means 'Cui's father heard the son's words and thought of one thing.'
  • 喜其智足卫身 - 喜 means to be happy, 其智 means his intelligence, 足卫身 means sufficient to protect himself. Together, it means 'happy that his intelligence is sufficient to protect himself.'
  • 不负父教 - 不 means not, 负 means to betray, and 父教 means father's teachings. Together, it means 'does not betray his father's teachings.'
  • 因慨然许之 - 因 means therefore, 慨然 means with a sigh, and 许之 means to agree. Together, it means 'therefore sighed and agreed.'
  • 草创婚仪 - 草创 means to hastily prepare, and 婚仪 means wedding ceremony. Together, it means 'hastily prepared the wedding ceremony.'
  • 令子迎妇归 - 令 means to order, 子 means the son, 迎妇 means to welcome the bride, and 归 means to return home. Together, it means 'ordered the son to welcome the bride home.'
  • 始合卺焉 - 始 means beginning, 合卺 means to share a wine cup, and 焉 is a particle indicating completion. Together, it means 'began to share a wine cup.'
  • 邻比闻十三完璧而归 - 邻比 means neighbors, 闻 means to hear, 完璧 means intact or whole, and 而归 means and returned. Together, it means 'neighbors heard that Thirteen returned intact.'
  • 且知念一为所伤 - 且 means also, 知 means to know, 念一 is a person's name, and 为所伤 means was injured. Together, it means 'also knew that Nianyi was injured.'
  • 病不能起 - 病 means to be ill, and 不能起 means unable to get up. Together, it means 'ill and unable to get up.'
  • 皆叹息以为异 - 皆 means all, 叹息 means to sigh, 以为 means to consider, and 异 means strange. Together, it means 'all sighed and considered it strange.'
  • 竟不疑女之自来 - 竟 means finally, 不疑 means did not doubt, and 女之自来 means that the woman came herself. Together, it means 'finally did not doubt that the woman came herself.'
  • 女事翁孝 - 女 means the woman, 事 means to serve, 翁 means father-in-law, and 孝 means filial piety. Together, it means 'the woman served her father-in-law with filial piety.'
  • 事夫顺 - 事 means to serve, 夫 means husband, and 顺 means obedient. Together, it means 'served her husband obediently.'
  • 理家有则 - 理 means to manage, 家 means household, and 有则 means with rules. Together, it means 'managed the household with rules.'
  • 且以药疗崔父之疾 - 且 means also, 以药 means with medicine, 疗 means to treat, and 崔父之疾 means Cui's father's illness. Together, it means 'also treated Cui's father's illness with medicine.'
  • 病良已 - 病 means illness, and 良已 means completely cured. Together, it means 'illness completely cured.'
  • 十三又出金置产 - 十三 is a person's name, 又 means again, 出金 means to spend money, and 置产 means to acquire property. Together, it means 'Thirteen again spent money to acquire property.'
  • 俨然素封 - 俨然 means solemnly, and 素封 means a wealthy person. Together, it means 'solemnly became a wealthy person.'
  • 夫妇承欢数载 - 夫妇 means husband and wife, 承欢 means to enjoy each other's company, and 数载 means several years. Together, it means 'husband and wife enjoyed each other's company for several years.'
  • 父殁 - 父 means father, and 殁 means to die. Together, it means 'father died.'
  • 遂弃其业 - 遂 means then, 弃 means to abandon, and 其业 means his business. Together, it means 'then abandoned his business.'
  • 不知所之 - 不知 means did not know, and 所之 means where he went. Together, it means 'did not know where he went.'
  • 唯念一尚在 - 唯 means only, 念一 is a person's name, and 尚在 means still alive. Together, it means 'only Nianyi is still alive.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 不矜十三之智，而矜十三之孝 - I do not boast of the wisdom of Shisan, but I boast of his filial piety.
  • 不为十三幸，而每为十三危 - I do not feel fortunate for Shisan, but I always feel worried for him.
  • 向使不遇授书之人，则十三已为砧上之肉 - If Shisan had not met the person who taught him, he would already have been like meat on the chopping block.
  • 虽智足自卫，又乌能出庖人手耶 - Even if he had enough intelligence to defend himself, how could he escape the hands of the cook?
  • 抑安知非天鉴其孝，阴令贤母为之造谋，俾其全璧归赵哉 - But who knows if it was not the heavens recognizing his filial piety, secretly instructing a virtuous mother to devise a plan, allowing him to return unharmed?
  • 故为贫家之子弟念，与其学保身之十三，母宁为不轻身之十三 - Therefore, for the children of poor families, it is better to learn from the example of Shisan who does not lightly risk his life, rather than from the example of Shisan who focuses on self-preservation.
  • 苟无十三之孝，念一必将饱饫秀色而归矣 - If Shisan did not have filial piety, he would surely return with his desires fulfilled.
  • 岂仅作门外汉，竟至焦头烂额乎 - How could he just remain an outsider, but end up in a disastrous situation?
  • 孝矣哉！十三。危矣哉！亦十三也。 - Filial piety indeed! Shisan. Danger indeed! Also Shisan.
  • 全璧归赵 - A reference to a historical story where a valuable object is returned intact, symbolizing a safe return.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 外史氏 - Refers to an external historian or commentator, often used in classical texts to provide commentary or reflection.

  • 砧上之肉 - A metaphor for someone who is about to be killed or sacrificed.
  • 庖人 - Refers to a cook or butcher, often used metaphorically to represent danger or harm.
  • 昌黎滕王之感 - A reference to the regret felt by Han Yu and Wang Bo, likely due to their inability to enjoy the beauty of nature.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 居恒辄谓卢曰 - 居恒 means 'always' or 'consistently,' and 辄谓 means 'would often say' or 'would frequently tell.'

  • 出宰百里 - 出宰 means 'to be appointed as an official,' and 百里 refers to a small administrative area.
  • 苟非名胜之邦斯已耳 - 苟非 means 'if not,' 名胜之邦 means 'famous and beautiful place,' and 斯已耳 means 'then it is enough.'
  • 脱遇六桥三竺之胜 - 脱遇 means 'if one encounters,' 六桥 refers to the six bridges in Hangzhou, and 三竺 refers to the three temples (Zhuji, Guoqing, and Zhegu).
  • 不能驾一叶之舟，与画船箫鼓竟逐于湖上 - 驾一叶之舟 means 'to sail a small boat,' 画船 means 'painted boat,' 箫鼓 means 'flutes and drums,' and 竟逐 means 'to compete' or 'to race.'
  • 不免唐突西子矣 - 不免 means 'cannot help but,' 唐突 means 'to intrude' or 'to offend,' and 西子 refers to Xi Shi, a famous beauty from ancient China.
  • 卢深颔其论 - 颔 means 'nod' or 'agree.'
  • 然亦密迩如兰亭，接壤如耶溪 - 密迩 means 'close to,' 兰亭 refers to the famous pavilion in Shaoxing, and 接壤 means 'bordering' or 'adjacent to,' 耶溪 refers to the Yexi River.
  • 犹以一官匏系 - 匏系 means 'to be bound by official duties.'
  • 即有淡妆浓抹者 - 淡妆浓抹 means 'lightly dressed or heavily made up,' often referring to beautiful women.
  • 可能驾言出游，以适我愿乎 - 驾言出游 means 'to go out for a drive,' and 以适我愿 means 'to satisfy my wish.'
  • 叟微笑曰 - 叟 means 'old man' or 'elder,' and 微笑 means 'smile.'
  • 君自不欲暇耳 - 自不欲暇 means 'you just don't want to take the time.'
  • 即以孤屿为家，以冷泉为室，以净慈灵隐为客座，以两峰一水为园亭 - 孤屿 means 'solitary island,' 冷泉 means 'cold spring,' 净慈灵隐 refers to the Jingci and Lingyin temples, 客座 means 'guest seat' or 'guest hall,' 两峰一水 means 'two peaks and one water,' and 园亭 means 'garden pavilion.'
  • 吾未见有废事也 - 吾未见 means 'I have not seen,' and 有废事 means 'anything being neglected.'
  • 湖船已具 - 湖船 means 'lake boat,' and 已具 means 'is ready.'
  • 来朝当与君遍游佳胜 - 来朝 means 'tomorrow morning,' 当 means 'will,' 与君 means 'with you,' 遍游 means 'to tour thoroughly,' and 佳胜 means 'beautiful places.'
